log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a UPX compressed PE32 executable, which matches the CAPA behavior. I should investigate the network activity to identify potential C2 servers.
The malware communicates with 136.243.204.158 over TCP port 443, which is likely a C2 server. I should investigate the registry changes to see if there are any persistence mechanisms.
There are no persistence mechanisms found in the registry. I should investigate the dropped files to see if there are any suspicious files written to disk.

The malware drops a file named AppVScripting.exe in multiple locations, which could be a payload or a dropper. I should investigate the process activity to see if there are any suspicious processes sp
The malware spawns a RegAsm.exe process, which is likely used to inject code into explorer.exe. I should investigate the highlighted API calls to see if they are related to any known TTPs.

The malware is packed with UPX and contains a resource section, which is consistent with the initial report. I should investigate the highlighted API calls to see if they are related to any known TTPs

The malware communicates with 2 unique IPs and 1 unique domain, and drops 3 files. It creates 9 processes and makes 2 highlighted API calls. I should now form a hypothesis based on the observed behavi
